title: flutter_local_notifications
objectives: Combine with FCM for hybrid flows

sections.markdown: `flutter_local_notifications` schedules and shows OS notifications from your app — reminders, alarms, timers — without a server. Different from FCM (which pushes from a server), though the two often combine.

Android channels are required (8.0+): create them once at startup with correct importance. Android 13+ requires POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime permission. iOS uses interruption levels (`passive`, `active`, `timeSensitive`, `critical`) — pick the right one.

Assuming exact alarms are free on Android 12+. `SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM` (or `USE_EXACT_ALARM`) permission is needed, and exact alarms are user-visible in system settings. Use inexact scheduling unless timing truly matters.

Use the `timezone` package (`tz.local`) for correct DST-aware scheduling. On iOS, register categories for actions (buttons). Handle app-launched-from-notification by reading `getNotificationAppLaunchDetails()`.

flashcards.back: A notification channel per category with an importance level.
Need SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM/USE_EXACT_ALARM; prefer inexact when possible.
passive, active, timeSensitive, critical — pick by user impact.

options: Wake up user via alarm always
quiz.explain: Local notifications don't need a server.

challenges.text: Show an immediate local notification with a channel.
Schedule a reminder for tomorrow at 9AM local time.
Combine FCM (delivery) + local notifications (display) with rich actions.

interviewQuestions: Exact vs inexact alarms on Android 12+?
